content: Just as human language learners consolidate vocabulary knowledge and correct usage through repeated explicit reflection exercises (a core part of high school English Unit 9 curriculum), LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the spaced, scaffolded reflection design from language education to improve their ability to correct logical errors and retain reliable problem-solving patterns. The orthographic variant "reflexion" (the older spelling of reflection) itself also acts as a "raw memory trace" that mirrors how uncurated self-reflection outputs are stored in an LLM's dynamic memory before being consolidated into usable knowledge.
